title: Europe Faces Dangerous Heatwave as Temperatures Climb
subtitle: Authorities across the continent issue emergency alerts as record-breaking heat disrupts daily life and public events.

summary.en: A severe heatwave is hitting large parts of Europe, leading to record-breaking temperatures and emergency red alerts. France has taken strict measures, including banning public alcohol consumption and closing hundreds of schools. The extreme weather is caused by a high-pressure system bringing hot air from the Sahara desert. Other countries, including Italy, Spain, and Germany, are also dealing with dangerous conditions and public safety concerns. Experts warn that these intense heat events are becoming more frequent and longer due to global climate change.
summary.zh: 一波嚴重的熱浪正襲擊歐洲大片地區，導致氣溫屢創新高並觸發緊急紅色警戒。法國已採取嚴格措施，包括禁止在公共場所飲酒以及關閉數百所學校。這波極端天氣是由一個將撒哈拉沙漠熱空氣帶來的強大高壓系統所引起。包括義大利、西班牙與德國在內的其他國家，也正處理危險的天氣狀況與公共安全疑慮。專家警告，由於全球氣候變遷，這些強烈的高溫事件正變得更加頻繁且持續時間更長。

paragraphs.en: A dangerous heatwave is currently moving across Europe, forcing governments to issue emergency warnings and change daily routines. The extreme weather, which began around the summer solstice, has pushed temperatures above 40 degrees Celsius in several countries. According to the BBC, this heat is being driven by hot air moving north from the Sahara desert. This air is trapped over Western and Central Europe by a strong high-pressure system, often called a "heat dome," which keeps the temperature rising day after day.
paragraphs.zh: 一波危險的熱浪目前正橫掃歐洲，迫使各國政府發布緊急警報並調整日常生活作息。這波極端天氣大約在夏至前後開始，已導致多國氣溫飆破攝氏四十度。根據英國廣播公司（BBC）報導，這波熱浪是由從撒哈拉沙漠向北移動的熱空氣所推動。這些熱空氣被一個強大的高壓系統困在西歐與中歐上空，該系統常被稱為「熱穹」，導致氣溫日復一日持續攀升。

paragraphs.en: France is experiencing some of the most severe conditions. According to The Guardian, more than a third of the country is under a "red alert," which is the highest level of danger. French officials have warned that these high temperatures will likely continue for the rest of the week. The situation is so serious that nearly 850 schools across the country were forced to close on Monday, while many others changed their schedules to protect students from the heat. The French rail network has also faced major problems, with SNCF chief Jean Castex noting that the heat can damage power lines and cause train tracks to expand, leading to the cancellation of dozens of intercity trains.
paragraphs.zh: 法國正經歷最嚴峻的情況。根據《衛報》（The Guardian）報導，法國超過三分之一的國土處於最高危險等級的「紅色警戒」狀態。法國官員警告，這種高溫天氣很可能會持續到本週結束。情況相當嚴重，週一全國有近八百五十所學校被迫關閉，許多其他學校也調整了課表以保護學生免受高溫影響。法國鐵路網也面臨重大問題，法國國家鐵路公司（SNCF）負責人卡斯泰（Jean Castex）指出，高溫可能會損壞電力線路並導致鐵軌膨脹，造成數十班城際列車被迫取消。

paragraphs.en: Public events have also been heavily affected. France’s annual music festival, the Fête de la Musique, took place despite the heat, but the government introduced strict rules to keep people safe. According to BBC News, authorities banned alcohol consumption in public places in areas under red alert. The government stated that this decision was made to reduce pressure on emergency services and hospitals, allowing medical staff to focus on helping the most vulnerable people. In Paris, the city government has kept parks and gardens open through the night to provide cool spaces for residents and tourists. Additionally, the city has installed over 1,300 public water fountains and partnered with local shops to provide free water to anyone who needs it.
paragraphs.zh: 公共活動也受到嚴重影響。法國年度音樂節「音樂節」（Fête de la Musique）儘管天氣炎熱仍照常舉行，但政府實施了嚴格規定以確保民眾安全。根據 BBC 新聞報導，當局禁止在紅色警戒地區的公共場所飲酒。政府表示，這項決定是為了減輕緊急服務單位與醫院的壓力，讓醫護人員能專注於協助最脆弱的族群。在巴黎，市政府將公園與花園全天候開放，為居民與遊客提供涼爽空間。此外，該市還安裝了超過一千三百座公共飲水台，並與當地商店合作，為有需要的人提供免費飲用水。

paragraphs.en: Other parts of Europe are facing similar challenges. According to Deutsche Welle, Italy has issued red alerts for eight major cities, including Milan, Florence, and Turin, where temperatures have stayed above 35 degrees Celsius. In Spain, the weather agency AEMET has warned that temperatures will likely reach 40 degrees across large parts of the country. Meanwhile, in Germany, the heat has been accompanied by severe storms. The Berlin Open tennis tournament had to be suspended after heavy rain and strong winds forced organizers to evacuate the grounds. German officials have also warned citizens to be very careful when swimming in rivers and lakes, as several people have sadly drowned or gone missing while trying to cool off in the water.
paragraphs.zh: 歐洲其他地區也面臨類似挑戰。根據德國之聲（Deutsche Welle）報導，義大利已對米蘭、佛羅倫斯與杜林等八大城市發布紅色警戒，當地氣溫持續維持在攝氏三十五度以上。在西班牙，氣象局（AEMET）警告，該國大片地區氣溫很可能達到四十度。與此同時，德國的高溫伴隨著強烈風暴。柏林網球公開賽因大雨與強風迫使主辦單位疏散場地人員而被迫暫停。德國官員也警告民眾在河流與湖泊游泳時務必格外小心，因為已有數人在試圖下水消暑時不幸溺斃或失蹤。

paragraphs.en: This is the second heatwave to hit Europe this year, and scientists are concerned about the long-term trend. According to reports from The Guardian, experts have stated that as the Earth continues to warm, extreme heat events will become more frequent, more intense, and last longer. These events are also starting earlier in the year and ending later. The World Health Organization’s Europe office has noted that heatwaves are deadly, with over 200,000 people across the continent dying from heat-related causes over the last four years.
paragraphs.zh: 這是今年襲擊歐洲的第二波熱浪，科學家對此長期趨勢感到擔憂。根據《衛報》報導，專家指出，隨著地球持續暖化，極端高溫事件將變得更頻繁、更強烈且持續時間更長。這些事件發生的時間也開始提早，結束的時間則延後。世界衛生組織（WHO）歐洲辦事處指出，熱浪致命性極高，過去四年歐洲大陸已有超過二十萬人死於與高溫相關的原因。

paragraphs.en: As the heat continues, government leaders are looking for ways to adapt. French Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu held emergency meetings to discuss how the country can better prepare for future heatwaves. For now, the advice from officials remains the same: stay hydrated, avoid unnecessary travel, and take great care during the hottest parts of the day. With the heatwave expected to last at least until the middle of the week, many Europeans are waiting for the temperatures to finally drop to safer levels.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著熱浪持續，各國政府領袖正尋求適應之道。法國總理勒科爾尼（Sébastien Lecornu）召開緊急會議，討論國家如何能為未來的熱浪做好更完善的準備。目前，官員的建議依然相同：保持水分充足、避免非必要的旅行，並在一天中最炎熱的時段格外小心。由於預計熱浪至少會持續到本週中期，許多歐洲民眾正等待氣溫最終降至較安全的水平。

keyTerms.definitionEn: Caused or influenced by a particular force or factor.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「駕駛」，這裡指天氣現象的成因。文中：According to the BBC, this heat is being driven by hot air moving north from the Sahara desert.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Kept in a specific place and unable to move or escape.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（陷阱），這裡形容空氣被高壓系統困住無法散去。文中：This air is trapped over Western and Central Europe by a strong high-pressure system, often called a "heat dome," which keeps the temperature rising day after day.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To become less hot or to lower one's body temperature.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「冷靜下來」，這裡指物理上的降溫。文中：German officials have also warned citizens to be very careful when swimming in rivers and lakes, as several people have sadly drowned or gone missing while trying to cool off in the water.

sources.name: BBC News
sources.title: Half of France under red heat alert as alcohol banned at street music festival

sources.name: The Guardian World
sources.title: France cancels events and restricts alcohol consumption amid brutal heatwave

sources.name: Deutsche Welle (DW) Top
sources.title: Severe heat wave sweeps Europe, triggering alerts and disruptions

quiz.question: What specific measure did the French government take during the Fête de la Musique to assist emergency services?

options.A: They canceled the festival entirely to prevent large crowds.
options.B: They prohibited the public consumption of alcohol in red alert areas.
options.C: They provided free medical check-ups at all festival entrances.
options.D: They restricted the festival to only night-time hours.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文章第三段明確指出，為了減輕緊急服務與醫院的負擔，政府在紅色警戒區內禁止了公共場合飲酒。選項 A 錯誤，因為音樂節照常舉行；選項 C 文中並未提及；選項 D 雖然提到巴黎開放公園供人避暑，但並非針對音樂節的具體限制措施。

quiz.question: Based on the information provided, why might the French rail network be struggling during this heatwave?

options.A: The heat has caused a shortage of train drivers who are unable to work.
options.B: The government has redirected all electricity to schools and hospitals.
options.C: The extreme temperatures are physically altering the infrastructure of the tracks.
options.D: The rail network is being used primarily to transport water to affected areas.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第二段，SNCF 的負責人指出高溫會導致火車軌道膨脹（expand）並損壞電線，進而導致列車停駛。選項 A 文中未提及司機缺勤；選項 B 是對資源分配的錯誤推論；選項 D 雖然聽起來合理，但文章中並未提到火車被用於運水，這屬於過度聯想。

quiz.question: In the phrase 'This air is trapped over Western and Central Europe by a strong high-pressure system', what does the word 'trapped' mean?

options.A: Captured and held in a confined space.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。在此語境中，trapped 形容熱空氣被高氣壓系統「困住」或「限制」在某個區域內，無法散去。選項 B 是指被欺騙，不符合物理現象；選項 C 與雲層遮蔽無關；選項 D 雖然有阻礙之意，但 trapped 強調的是「無法逃脫」的封閉狀態，而非單純的減速。

quiz.question: What is the primary message regarding the current European heatwave as presented in the article?

options.A: The heatwave is a temporary inconvenience that will end once the summer solstice passes.
options.B: European countries are successfully managing the heatwave with no long-term negative effects.
options.C: The heatwave is a localized event affecting only France and Italy.
options.D: The heatwave is part of a concerning long-term trend of increasingly frequent and intense extreme weather.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 D。文章第五段明確提到，科學家與專家指出，隨著地球暖化，極端高溫事件將變得更頻繁、更強烈且持續時間更長。選項 A 錯誤，因為文章強調其長期趨勢；選項 B 過於樂觀，忽視了死亡人數與嚴重影響；選項 C 錯誤，因為文章也提到了德國與其他歐洲地區受到的影響。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
